D9 BiologyL5 EvaluateReference

Peacock Feather — 2D Photonic Crystal

peacock tail barbules contain a 2D lattice of melanin rods in keratin; different lattice spacings in the same feather give blue, green, copper, and yellow eye-spot zones

Zi 2003 measured a 2D square lattice of melanin rods (diameter 100 nm) in keratin; the lattice constant varies regionally across the feather — 140 nm gives blue, 165 nm gives green, 185 nm gives copper. Photonic band-gap principle: only light at the matching wavelength reflects coherently.

Drag lattice constant; the reflection colour cycles blue → green → copper.
